summary: V6-F request-side intercept (L-027 pattern) CONFIRMED. After PII submit + Ctrl+Shift+R hard reload, the new H2 GET /chat?t=...&wfv=chat was intercepted BEFORE upstream HEADERS were sent. Engine emitted a clean H2-framed warning HTML body (200 OK, 1.1 kB, Content-Type text/html, X-Apf-Page-Inject:1). Browser rendered the Korean security warning with tab title '보안 정책 - 접근 차단'. NO '(failed)' Doc row for the post-reload navigation, NO Service Worker fallback. notion warning pipeline DONE.

test_pc_workflow.step1_open_chat: OK - https://www.notion.so/chat?t=... loaded, logged in, DevTools (Console+Network) open
test_pc_workflow.step2_pii_submit: OK - prompt '내 주민등록번호는 880101-1234567 인데 이걸로 무엇을 알 수 있어?' submitted via prompt input
test_pc_workflow.step3_v6a_ndjson_block: OK - inferred from console errors (markInferenceTranscriptSeen 404, HttpRequestError) following submit; chat stream was severed by V6-A NDJSON-side block
test_pc_workflow.step4_hard_reload: OK - Ctrl+Shift+R triggered, new H2 connection forced
test_pc_workflow.step5_v6f_request_side_intercept: OK - GET /chat?t=...&wfv=chat returned 200 with body (1.1 kB) carrying X-Apf-Page-Inject:1 marker — request-side intercept fired BEFORE upstream HEADERS
test_pc_workflow.step6_h2_framed_warning_html: OK - clean text/html;charset=UTF-8 body served over H2 (no upstream HEADERS conflict, no SW fallback)
test_pc_workflow.step7_doc_row_check: OK - critical row 'chat?t=...' shows 200 OK / 1.1 kB / 6 ms (NOT '(failed)' 0.0 kB)
test_pc_workflow.step8_render_and_title: OK - tab title '보안 정책 - 접근 차단', visible Korean warning '보안 경고 / 민감정보가 포함된 요청은 보안 정책에 의해 차단되었습니다. (This request was blocked due to sensitive information.)'
test_pc_workflow.step9_screenshots: OK - before.png, after_submit.png, after_reload.png, network.png, console.png saved to files/722/

other_doc_rows_observed: chat?t=... (failed) 0.0 kB / 42 ms — pre-reload SPA fetch attempt (V6-A blocked stream tear-down)
chat?t=... (failed) 0.0 kB / 95 ms — pre-reload SPA fetch attempt (V6-A blocked stream tear-down)
chat?t=... 200 (Service Worker) / 77 ms — pre-reload SW fallback served stale shell
chat?t=... 200 / 1.1 kB / 6 ms — POST-RELOAD V6-F intercept (the critical row)
network_observations.console_errors_after_reload: 2 errors only (favicon.ico 404 unrelated to APF). Reduced from ~75 errors pre-reload — the warning page is a clean static HTML, no SPA framework fighting the engine.

dom_scan.title: 보안 정책 - 접근 차단
dom_scan.bodySnippet: ⚠️ 보안 경고\n민감정보가 포함된 요청은 보안 정책에 의해 차단되었습니다.\n(This request was blocked due to sensitive information.)
dom_scan.render_method: Full document replacement (warning HTML served as the chat?t=... document body — not chat-bubble injection)
dom_scan.notes: xApfInject DOM check returned false because the marker is an HTTP RESPONSE HEADER (X-Apf-Page-Inject:1), not a meta tag. Header marker confirmed via Network panel.

comparison_vs_721.721_pattern: V6-E response-side trigger (on_http2_response). Engine attempted substitution AFTER upstream HEADERS already in flight to browser → Chrome saw stream tear-down → ServiceWorker fallback served stale shell → no warning rendered → Doc row showed '(failed) 0.0 kB'.
comparison_vs_721.722_pattern: V6-F request-side trigger (L-027 pattern, same as V7-E for perplexity). Engine intercepts the GET BEFORE the request reaches the notion server — no upstream HEADERS exist to conflict, so the engine freely emits its own H2-framed HEADERS+DATA frames. Browser receives a clean 200 response with full body and renders normally.
key_diff_observations: 721: Doc row was '(failed)' 0.0 kB — V6-E race lost to upstream HEADERS
722: Doc row is 200 OK 1.1 kB — V6-F preempts upstream entirely
721: ServiceWorker fallback rendered stale chat shell (no warning)
722: No SW fallback — direct H2 response carries the warning HTML
722: tab title changed to '보안 정책 - 접근 차단' (warning HTML <title>)
722: koreanWarning DOM check = true, full Korean+English bilingual message
722: New X-Apf-Page-Inject:1 response header marks the intercepted response (forensic signal for post-mortem)
comparison_vs_721.regression_risk: None. Other Doc rows (aif-production.html, authSync) returned 200 normally — V6-F is targeted only at the matched chat URL. Pre-reload SPA fetches still get '(failed)' as expected (V6-A NDJSON layer is unchanged), but post-reload navigation now succeeds with warning.

next_action_recommendation: Mark notion service warning pipeline as DONE in genai-apf-pipeline. V6-F (request-side intercept / L-027 pattern) is the canonical solution for SPA targets where upstream HEADERS race kills V6-E response-side substitution. Consider promoting L-027 pattern to default for all future SPA service registrations. No further test runs required for notion.
